content.content:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	Guidance for Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working in this repository.
4	
5	## Current focus
6	
7	We are **closing the API and frontend architecture first**. Sync between
8	this API and the external system is **deferred** and out of scope for
9	now — do not start sync work, do not add sync packages, do not add
10	sync-related abstractions. If a request seems to push toward sync,
11	stop and ask.
12	
13	Order of work, in this order, no jumping ahead:
14	
15	1. Lock down the API architecture (this repo).
16	2. Close pending items on the React frontend (separate repo).
17	3. Then, and only then, design and implement sync.
18	
19	## Commands
20	
21	```bash
22	# Build (warnings are errors — see Directory.Build.props)
23	dotnet build
24	
25	# Run API (SQL Server connection string in appsettings.Development.json)
26	dotnet run --project TodoApi
27	
28	# All tests
29	dotnet test
30	
31	# Single test class / method
32	dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TodoListsControllerTests"
33	dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TodoListsControllerTests.GetTodoList_WhenCalled_ReturnsTodoListList"
34	
35	# Format (CSharpier)
36	dotnet csharpier .
37	
38	# EF Core migrations (run from repo root; project flag points at the API)
39	dotnet ef migrations add <Name> --project TodoApi
40	dotnet ef database update --project TodoApi
41	```
42	
43	## Treat the existing code as starter scaffolding, not a base
44	
45	The code currently in `TodoApi/` (controllers calling `TodoContext`
46	directly, anemic `Models/`, `Dtos/` folder, etc.) is the **starter
47	layout from the interview template**. It is **not** the base we are
48	building on. Do not extend it, do not preserve its shape, do not add
49	new endpoints next to the existing ones.
50	
51	The target architecture below replaces it. Existing files get **moved,
52	rewritten, or deleted** as each module is introduced. Migrations and
53	the `TodoContext` get reorganized under the first module's
54	`Infrastructure` and renamed accordingly. When in doubt about what to
55	keep, the answer is: keep the build settings (`Directory.Build.props`,
56	`Directory.Packages.props`, `.editorconfig`, solution file) and
57	replace everything else.
58	
59	External integration tests live at
60	<https://github.com/crunchloop/interview-tests> and run against the API
61	over HTTP — the public contract those tests expect (resource URLs,
62	payload shapes, status codes) is the constraint that the new
63	implementation must still satisfy. Read those tests before reshaping
64	endpoints.
65	
66	## Target architecture
67	
68	We are migrating the codebase toward a **modular monolith** organized
69	by **Clean Architecture** layers, with **DDD** building blocks inside
70	each module and **CQRS** as the application-layer pattern. Adopt these
71	incrementally, one module at a time. Do **not** rewrite the whole repo
72	in one PR — work module-by-module and document each step with an ADR
73	(see below).
74	
75	### Layering (per module)
76	
77	```
78	TodoApi.Modules.<ModuleName>/
79	  Domain/           — entities, value objects, domain events, aggregates,
80	                       domain services, repository interfaces
81	  Application/      — commands, queries, handlers, DTOs, validators,
82	                       application services, port interfaces
83	  Infrastructure/   — EF Core configurations, repository implementations,
84	                       integrations, persistence
85	  Api/ (optional)   — controllers / endpoints exposing this module
86	```
87	
88	Dependency rule, no exceptions:
89	`Api → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure`. Domain depends on
90	nothing. Infrastructure depends on Domain to implement ports.
91	Application orchestrates and depends on Domain. Api depends on
92	Application (and DTOs, never on Domain entities directly).
93	
94	### Modular monolith boundaries
95	
96	- One folder/project per bounded context. Start with `TodoLists` as the
97	  first module; do not pre-create empty modules.
98	- Cross-module communication goes through **application-level
99	  contracts** (commands/queries/events), never by reaching into another
100	  module's `Domain` or `Infrastructure`.
101	- Shared kernel is allowed only for genuinely shared primitives
102	  (`Result<T>`, `Error`, base `Entity`/`ValueObject`, ids). Keep it
103	  small. If you are tempted to put a domain concept there, it belongs
104	  in a module.
105	- Use **Scrutor** (already in `Directory.Packages.props`) to scan and
106	  register handlers/services per module — avoid one giant DI
107	  registration block.
108	
109	### DDD building blocks
110	
111	- **Entities** with identity and behavior (no anemic models — methods
112	  live on the entity, not in services).
113	- **Value objects** for concepts without identity; immutable; equality
114	  by value.
115	- **Aggregates** with a single root that enforces invariants.
116	  Repositories are per aggregate root, not per table.
117	- **Domain events** raised from aggregates; dispatched by the
118	  application layer after the unit of work commits.
119	- **Repositories** are interfaces in `Domain`; EF Core implementations
120	  live in `Infrastructure`.
121	- **Specifications** for non-trivial queries that belong to the domain
122	  vocabulary.
123	
124	### CQRS with Wolverine
125	
126	We use **Wolverine** (<https://wolverinefx.net>) as our CQRS
127	dispatcher and in-process message bus. Packaged as `WolverineFx` in
128	`Directory.Packages.props`. See ADR-0008 (which supersedes ADR-0004's
129	Kommand choice).
130	
131	Conventions:
132	
133	- `Application/Commands/<UseCase>/` contains the command record, the
134	  handler class, and (optionally) a validator. One folder per use
135	  case.
136	- `Application/Queries/<UseCase>/` mirrors the same shape for reads.
137	- Commands and queries are plain records — **no marker interface**.
138	  Wolverine discovers handlers by convention: a class whose name ends
139	  in `Handler` with a `Handle` / `HandleAsync` method taking the
140	  message type. Keep handlers in `Application/Commands|Queries/…`
141	  so the auto-scan picks them up.
142	- Handlers return `Result` / `Result<T>` — no throwing for expected
143	  failures (validation, not-found, conflict). Throw only for truly
144	  exceptional conditions (ADR-0006).
145	- Handlers are **thin POCOs**: load aggregate(s), call domain methods,
146	  persist, return. Business rules live in the domain, not the handler.
147	- Controllers/endpoints translate HTTP → command/query, dispatch via
148	  `IMessageBus.InvokeAsync<T>(...)`, translate result → HTTP via the
149	  shared `Result → HTTP` helper that renders failures as
150	  `ProblemDetails` (see "API error responses" below). No business
151	  logic in controllers.
152	- Cross-cutting concerns use **Wolverine middleware**:
153	  - FluentValidation via `WolverineFx.FluentValidation`, wired so that
154	    validator failures become a `Result` with
155	    `Error.Category = Validation`. Do **not** throw `ValidationException`
156	    from handlers or middleware (ADR-0006).
157	  - Transactions via Wolverine's `TransactionalMiddleware` /
158	    `[Transactional]` once a handler crosses aggregates.
159	  - Logging/tracing via Wolverine's OpenTelemetry support (ADR-0009).
160	- Domain events raised by aggregates (ADR-0003) are published via
161	  Wolverine (`CascadeMessages` on the handler) after
162	  `SaveChangesAsync` returns — no hand-rolled dispatcher.
163	
164	### Entity Framework Core
165	
166	- One `DbContext` per module, scoped to that module's aggregates.
167	- Configuration goes in `Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/`
168	  using `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>`. No Fluent API in `OnModelCreating`
169	  beyond `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly`.
170	- Migrations belong to the module that owns the `DbContext`.
171	- `InMemory` provider stays for unit tests. Integration tests that need
172	  real SQL semantics go against SQL Server (devcontainer provisions it).
173	- Repository implementations expose **aggregate-shaped** APIs, not
174	  generic `IRepository<T>`. Avoid leaking `IQueryable` outside
175	  `Infrastructure`.
176	
177	### API error responses (RFC 7807 ProblemDetails)
178	
179	All HTTP failure responses are rendered as **`ProblemDetails`** /
180	`ValidationProblemDetails` (`application/problem+json`, RFC 7807).
181	This is the only error format the API speaks — no ad-hoc
182	`{ "error": "..." }` envelopes, no plain-text error bodies.
183	
184	Conventions:
185	
186	- Enable framework support in `Program.cs` via `AddProblemDetails`,
187	  `UseExceptionHandler`, `UseStatusCodePages`. Do not hand-roll
188	  middleware that duplicates that.
189	- A single shared helper (`result.ToActionResult()` for controllers /
190	  `result.ToHttpResult()` for Minimal APIs) maps
191	  `Result.Error.Category` → HTTP status:
192	  `Validation`→400, `Unauthorized`→401, `Forbidden`→403,
193	  `NotFound`→404, `Conflict`→409, `Unexpected`→500.
194	- Body fields: `type` (stable URI per error code, even if it does not
195	  resolve), `title`, `status`, `detail` (= `Error.Message`),
196	  `instance` (request path, automatic), and the extensions `code`
197	  (= `Error.Code` from the Result model) and `traceId`.
198	- Validation errors use `ValidationProblemDetails` with field-keyed
199	  `errors`.
200	- The global exception handler renders unhandled exceptions as a
201	  generic 500 `ProblemDetails`. **No** stack traces or internal
202	  details in the response body — those go to the log, correlated by
203	  `traceId`.
204	- Controllers do not build `ProblemDetails` by hand; they call the
205	  shared helper.
206	
207	See ADR-0007.
208	
209	### Logging (Serilog)
210	
211	Logging goes through **Serilog** (`Serilog.AspNetCore` + Console /
212	File / Seq sinks). See ADR-0009. Highlights:
213	
214	- `Program.cs` calls `UseSerilog((ctx, cfg) => cfg.ReadFrom.Configuration(ctx.Configuration))`.
215	  All sink/enricher configuration lives in `appsettings*.json` under
216	  a `Serilog` section — do not hard-code sinks in C#.
217	- Required enrichers: `FromLogContext`, `WithMachineName`,
218	  `WithEnvironmentName`. The `TraceId` propagated via `LogContext`
219	  is the same one rendered in `ProblemDetails.traceId` (ADR-0007),
220	  so logs and error responses pivot against each other.
221	- Sinks: Console (dev loop), rolling File at `logs/todoapi-.log`
222	  (30-day retention), Seq at `http://localhost:5341` in the
223	  devcontainer. Seq is an optional dev tool — writes must fail
224	  silently, never crash the app.
225	- Use `ILogger<T>` in consumers. Do not reference `Log.Logger` or
226	  Serilog types outside the composition root.
227	- Do **not** log request/response bodies by default. Adding
228	  per-endpoint body logging requires explicit scope + redaction.
229	
230	### Patterns: use them where they pay rent
231	
232	Apply patterns when the requirement justifies them. Pick the *simplest*
233	thing that satisfies the requirement and the architectural rules above.
234	Common ones we expect to use:
235	
236	- Result/Either for error returns from handlers
237	- Specification for reusable query predicates
238	- Domain events + outbox if/when sync gets added (later)
239	- Decorator (via Scrutor) for cross-cutting concerns on handlers
240	  (logging, validation, transactions)
241	
242	Do **not** add patterns "for symmetry" or "for the future." If only one
243	handler needs validation, validate inline; introduce the pipeline
244	decorator when the second handler appears.
245	
246	## Standards
247	
248	### `.editorconfig` is the source of truth
249	
250	`.editorconfig` at repo root drives style and analyzer severity. If
251	guidance here ever conflicts with `.editorconfig`, follow
252	`.editorconfig` and update this file.
253	
254	Highlights worth remembering:
255	
256	- `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true` and `CodeAnalysisTreatWarningsAsErrors=true`
257	  in `Directory.Build.props` — analyzer warnings break the build.
258	- `AnalysisMode=All` with `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp` enabled.
259	- `var` is required (`csharp_style_var_*` = error).
260	- No `this.` qualification (`dotnet_style_qualification_for_*` = error).
261	- Use language keywords (`int`, not `Int32`).
262	- Expression-bodied properties/indexers/accessors required; methods and
263	  constructors stay block-bodied.
264	- Pattern matching preferred over `is`-cast / `as`-null-check (error).
265	- CA1040 (empty interfaces) is disabled. The original justification
266	  was CQRS marker interfaces; Wolverine does not require them
267	  (ADR-0008), so the exemption is dormant rather than load-bearing.
268	  Leave it disabled — re-enabling is out of scope for the Wolverine
269	  migration and would need its own ADR.
270	- Several CA/Sonar rules are downgraded to `suggestion` for incremental
271	  cleanup. Do not silence new rules without an ADR.
272	
273	### Package management
274	
275	- Versions live centrally in `Directory.Packages.props`
276	  (`ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true`). Project files reference
277	  packages without versions.
278	- When adding a package, update `Directory.Packages.props` first.
279	
280	## Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
281	
282	Any architectural choice — module boundaries, picking a library,
283	choosing a persistence strategy, introducing a cross-cutting pattern —
284	gets an ADR **before** the code lands.
285	
286	### Location and format
287	
288	```
289	docs/adr/
290	  0001-record-architecture-decisions.md
291	  0002-adopt-clean-architecture-and-modular-monolith.md
292	  0003-cqrs-with-kommand.md
293	  ...
294	```
295	
296	Use the **MADR** template (Markdown ADR), short form:
297	
298	```markdown
299	# <NNNN> - <Title>
300	
301	- Status: proposed | accepted | superseded by ADR-XXXX | deprecated
302	- Date: YYYY-MM-DD
303	- Deciders: <names>
304	
305	## Context and problem statement
306	What is the situation? What forces are at play?
307	
308	## Decision drivers
309	- <driver 1>
310	- <driver 2>
311	
312	## Considered options
313	- Option A
314	- Option B
315	- Option C
316	
317	## Decision outcome
318	Chosen option: "<A>", because <justification>.
319	
320	### Consequences
321	- Positive: ...
322	- Negative: ...
323	- Neutral: ...
324	
325	## Links
326	- Related ADRs, RFCs, issues, docs
327	```
328	
329	### Rules for ADRs
330	
331	- Numbered sequentially, never re-numbered. To overturn one, write a new
332	  ADR with status `accepted` that marks the old one `superseded by ADR-NNNN`.
333	- One decision per ADR. If a PR carries two decisions, write two ADRs.
334	- Keep them short — two screens max. If you need more, link out.
335	- ADR-0001 is always "Record architecture decisions" (the meta-ADR
336	  declaring we use ADRs). Add it as the first thing.
337	
338	### When to write one
339	
340	Write an ADR for: introducing/removing a library, changing a layer
341	boundary, picking a persistence pattern, adopting a cross-cutting
342	pattern (validation pipeline, outbox, auth scheme), changing how
343	modules communicate. Do **not** write ADRs for routine code — naming,
344	small refactors, bug fixes.
345	
346	### Existing ADRs
347	
348	The current architectural baseline is recorded in `docs/adr/`. Read
349	these before proposing changes that touch any of these areas:
350	
351	| #    | Title                                                        | What it locks in                                        |
352	|------|--------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
353	| 0001 | Record architecture decisions                                | Use MADR short form, numbered, in `docs/adr/`           |
354	| 0002 | Adopt Clean Architecture and Modular Monolith                | Per-module `Domain/Application/Infrastructure/Api`      |
355	| 0003 | Use DDD building blocks inside each module                   | Entities, value objects, aggregates, repositories       |
356	| 0004 | ~~CQRS as application pattern, dispatched via Kommand~~      | Superseded by ADR-0008 (Wolverine)                      |
357	| 0005 | Entity Framework Core for persistence, one DbContext / module| Per-module `DbContext`, migrations and configurations   |
358	| 0006 | Use `Result<T>` for expected failures                        | No exceptions for validation/not-found/conflict         |
359	| 0007 | Use RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails` for HTTP error responses       | Single error format, mapped from `Error.Category`       |
360	| 0008 | CQRS dispatched via Wolverine (supersedes ADR-0004)          | WolverineFx for CQRS + in-process messaging; no markers |
361	| 0009 | Serilog for structured logging                               | `Serilog.AspNetCore` + Console / File / Seq sinks       |
362	| 0010 | Use UUIDv7 (Guid v7) for aggregate identifiers               | `GuidV7.NewGuid()` today; `Guid.CreateVersion7()` on net9+ |
363	| 0011 | Unit testing conventions for handlers and validators         | Bogus + immutable Builders + `AsyncLifetimeBase` (EF InMemory) |
364	
365	When you write the next ADR, increment the number, add a row to this
366	table in the same PR, and link it from any related sections above.
367	
368	## What not to do
369	
370	- Do not start sync work or add sync-shaped abstractions yet.
371	- Do not introduce a new pattern without an ADR.
372	- Do not silence analyzer rules to make a warning go away — fix the
373	  code, or write an ADR explaining why the rule is being downgraded.
374	- Do not put business logic in controllers or handlers — push it to the
375	  domain.
376	- Do not create empty modules / projects "for later." Add structure
377	  when a real use case needs it.
378	- Do not bypass `Directory.Packages.props` by hard-coding versions in
379	  a `.csproj`.
380	- Do not preserve the starter `Controllers/`, `Models/`, `Dtos/`, or
381	  `Data/TodoContext.cs` as-is. They are scaffolding; replace them as
382	  the new modules come in.
383

# CLAUDE.md

Guidance for Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working in this repository.

## Current focus

We are **closing the API and frontend architecture first**. Sync between
this API and the external system is **deferred** and out of scope for
now — do not start sync work, do not add sync packages, do not add
sync-related abstractions. If a request seems to push toward sync,
stop and ask.

Order of work, in this order, no jumping ahead:

1. Lock down the API architecture (this repo).
2. Close pending items on the React frontend (separate repo).
3. Then, and only then, design and implement sync.

## Commands

```bash
# Build (warnings are errors — see Directory.Build.props)
dotnet build

# Run API (SQL Server connection string in appsettings.Development.json)
dotnet run --project TodoApi

# All tests
dotnet test

# Single test class / method
dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TodoListsControllerTests"
dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TodoListsControllerTests.GetTodoList_WhenCalled_ReturnsTodoListList"

# Format (CSharpier)
dotnet csharpier .

# EF Core migrations (run from repo root; project flag points at the API)
dotnet ef migrations add <Name> --project TodoApi
dotnet ef database update --project TodoApi
```

## Treat the existing code as starter scaffolding, not a base

The code currently in `TodoApi/` (controllers calling `TodoContext`
directly, anemic `Models/`, `Dtos/` folder, etc.) is the **starter
layout from the interview template**. It is **not** the base we are
building on. Do not extend it, do not preserve its shape, do not add
new endpoints next to the existing ones.

The target architecture below replaces it. Existing files get **moved,
rewritten, or deleted** as each module is introduced. Migrations and
the `TodoContext` get reorganized under the first module's
`Infrastructure` and renamed accordingly. When in doubt about what to
keep, the answer is: keep the build settings (`Directory.Build.props`,
`Directory.Packages.props`, `.editorconfig`, solution file) and
replace everything else.

External integration tests live at
<https://github.com/crunchloop/interview-tests> and run against the API
over HTTP — the public contract those tests expect (resource URLs,
payload shapes, status codes) is the constraint that the new
implementation must still satisfy. Read those tests before reshaping
endpoints.

## Target architecture

We are migrating the codebase toward a **modular monolith** organized
by **Clean Architecture** layers, with **DDD** building blocks inside
each module and **CQRS** as the application-layer pattern. Adopt these
incrementally, one module at a time. Do **not** rewrite the whole repo
in one PR — work module-by-module and document each step with an ADR
(see below).

### Layering (per module)

```
TodoApi.Modules.<ModuleName>/
  Domain/           — entities, value objects, domain events, aggregates,
                       domain services, repository interfaces
  Application/      — commands, queries, handlers, DTOs, validators,
                       application services, port interfaces
  Infrastructure/   — EF Core configurations, repository implementations,
                       integrations, persistence
  Api/ (optional)   — controllers / endpoints exposing this module
```

Dependency rule, no exceptions:
`Api → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure`. Domain depends on
nothing. Infrastructure depends on Domain to implement ports.
Application orchestrates and depends on Domain. Api depends on
Application (and DTOs, never on Domain entities directly).

### Modular monolith boundaries

- One folder/project per bounded context. Start with `TodoLists` as the
  first module; do not pre-create empty modules.
- Cross-module communication goes through **application-level
  contracts** (commands/queries/events), never by reaching into another
  module's `Domain` or `Infrastructure`.
- Shared kernel is allowed only for genuinely shared primitives
  (`Result<T>`, `Error`, base `Entity`/`ValueObject`, ids). Keep it
  small. If you are tempted to put a domain concept there, it belongs
  in a module.
- Use **Scrutor** (already in `Directory.Packages.props`) to scan and
  register handlers/services per module — avoid one giant DI
  registration block.

### DDD building blocks

- **Entities** with identity and behavior (no anemic models — methods
  live on the entity, not in services).
- **Value objects** for concepts without identity; immutable; equality
  by value.
- **Aggregates** with a single root that enforces invariants.
  Repositories are per aggregate root, not per table.
- **Domain events** raised from aggregates; dispatched by the
  application layer after the unit of work commits.
- **Repositories** are interfaces in `Domain`; EF Core implementations
  live in `Infrastructure`.
- **Specifications** for non-trivial queries that belong to the domain
  vocabulary.

### CQRS with Wolverine

We use **Wolverine** (<https://wolverinefx.net>) as our CQRS
dispatcher and in-process message bus. Packaged as `WolverineFx` in
`Directory.Packages.props`. See ADR-0008 (which supersedes ADR-0004's
Kommand choice).

Conventions:

- `Application/Commands/<UseCase>/` contains the command record, the
  handler class, and (optionally) a validator. One folder per use
  case.
- `Application/Queries/<UseCase>/` mirrors the same shape for reads.
- Commands and queries are plain records — **no marker interface**.
  Wolverine discovers handlers by convention: a class whose name ends
  in `Handler` with a `Handle` / `HandleAsync` method taking the
  message type. Keep handlers in `Application/Commands|Queries/…`
  so the auto-scan picks them up.
- Handlers return `Result` / `Result<T>` — no throwing for expected
  failures (validation, not-found, conflict). Throw only for truly
  exceptional conditions (ADR-0006).
- Handlers are **thin POCOs**: load aggregate(s), call domain methods,
  persist, return. Business rules live in the domain, not the handler.
- Controllers/endpoints translate HTTP → command/query, dispatch via
  `IMessageBus.InvokeAsync<T>(...)`, translate result → HTTP via the
  shared `Result → HTTP` helper that renders failures as
  `ProblemDetails` (see "API error responses" below). No business
  logic in controllers.
- Cross-cutting concerns use **Wolverine middleware**:
  - FluentValidation via `WolverineFx.FluentValidation`, wired so that
    validator failures become a `Result` with
    `Error.Category = Validation`. Do **not** throw `ValidationException`
    from handlers or middleware (ADR-0006).
  - Transactions via Wolverine's `TransactionalMiddleware` /
    `[Transactional]` once a handler crosses aggregates.
  - Logging/tracing via Wolverine's OpenTelemetry support (ADR-0009).
- Domain events raised by aggregates (ADR-0003) are published via
  Wolverine (`CascadeMessages` on the handler) after
  `SaveChangesAsync` returns — no hand-rolled dispatcher.

### Entity Framework Core

- One `DbContext` per module, scoped to that module's aggregates.
- Configuration goes in `Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/`
  using `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>`. No Fluent API in `OnModelCreating`
  beyond `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly`.
- Migrations belong to the module that owns the `DbContext`.
- `InMemory` provider stays for unit tests. Integration tests that need
  real SQL semantics go against SQL Server (devcontainer provisions it).
- Repository implementations expose **aggregate-shaped** APIs, not
  generic `IRepository<T>`. Avoid leaking `IQueryable` outside
  `Infrastructure`.

### API error responses (RFC 7807 ProblemDetails)

All HTTP failure responses are rendered as **`ProblemDetails`** /
`ValidationProblemDetails` (`application/problem+json`, RFC 7807).
This is the only error format the API speaks — no ad-hoc
`{ "error": "..." }` envelopes, no plain-text error bodies.

Conventions:

- Enable framework support in `Program.cs` via `AddProblemDetails`,
  `UseExceptionHandler`, `UseStatusCodePages`. Do not hand-roll
  middleware that duplicates that.
- A single shared helper (`result.ToActionResult()` for controllers /
  `result.ToHttpResult()` for Minimal APIs) maps
  `Result.Error.Category` → HTTP status:
  `Validation`→400, `Unauthorized`→401, `Forbidden`→403,
  `NotFound`→404, `Conflict`→409, `Unexpected`→500.
- Body fields: `type` (stable URI per error code, even if it does not
  resolve), `title`, `status`, `detail` (= `Error.Message`),
  `instance` (request path, automatic), and the extensions `code`
  (= `Error.Code` from the Result model) and `traceId`.
- Validation errors use `ValidationProblemDetails` with field-keyed
  `errors`.
- The global exception handler renders unhandled exceptions as a
  generic 500 `ProblemDetails`. **No** stack traces or internal
  details in the response body — those go to the log, correlated by
  `traceId`.
- Controllers do not build `ProblemDetails` by hand; they call the
  shared helper.

See ADR-0007.

### Logging (Serilog)

Logging goes through **Serilog** (`Serilog.AspNetCore` + Console /
File / Seq sinks). See ADR-0009. Highlights:

- `Program.cs` calls `UseSerilog((ctx, cfg) => cfg.ReadFrom.Configuration(ctx.Configuration))`.
  All sink/enricher configuration lives in `appsettings*.json` under
  a `Serilog` section — do not hard-code sinks in C#.
- Required enrichers: `FromLogContext`, `WithMachineName`,
  `WithEnvironmentName`. The `TraceId` propagated via `LogContext`
  is the same one rendered in `ProblemDetails.traceId` (ADR-0007),
  so logs and error responses pivot against each other.
- Sinks: Console (dev loop), rolling File at `logs/todoapi-.log`
  (30-day retention), Seq at `http://localhost:5341` in the
  devcontainer. Seq is an optional dev tool — writes must fail
  silently, never crash the app.
- Use `ILogger<T>` in consumers. Do not reference `Log.Logger` or
  Serilog types outside the composition root.
- Do **not** log request/response bodies by default. Adding
  per-endpoint body logging requires explicit scope + redaction.

### Patterns: use them where they pay rent

Apply patterns when the requirement justifies them. Pick the *simplest*
thing that satisfies the requirement and the architectural rules above.
Common ones we expect to use:

- Result/Either for error returns from handlers
- Specification for reusable query predicates
- Domain events + outbox if/when sync gets added (later)
- Decorator (via Scrutor) for cross-cutting concerns on handlers
  (logging, validation, transactions)

Do **not** add patterns "for symmetry" or "for the future." If only one
handler needs validation, validate inline; introduce the pipeline
decorator when the second handler appears.

## Standards

### `.editorconfig` is the source of truth

`.editorconfig` at repo root drives style and analyzer severity. If
guidance here ever conflicts with `.editorconfig`, follow
`.editorconfig` and update this file.

Highlights worth remembering:

- `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true` and `CodeAnalysisTreatWarningsAsErrors=true`
  in `Directory.Build.props` — analyzer warnings break the build.
- `AnalysisMode=All` with `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp` enabled.
- `var` is required (`csharp_style_var_*` = error).
- No `this.` qualification (`dotnet_style_qualification_for_*` = error).
- Use language keywords (`int`, not `Int32`).
- Expression-bodied properties/indexers/accessors required; methods and
  constructors stay block-bodied.
- Pattern matching preferred over `is`-cast / `as`-null-check (error).
- CA1040 (empty interfaces) is disabled. The original justification
  was CQRS marker interfaces; Wolverine does not require them
  (ADR-0008), so the exemption is dormant rather than load-bearing.
  Leave it disabled — re-enabling is out of scope for the Wolverine
  migration and would need its own ADR.
- Several CA/Sonar rules are downgraded to `suggestion` for incremental
  cleanup. Do not silence new rules without an ADR.

### Package management

- Versions live centrally in `Directory.Packages.props`
  (`ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true`). Project files reference
  packages without versions.
- When adding a package, update `Directory.Packages.props` first.

## Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

Any architectural choice — module boundaries, picking a library,
choosing a persistence strategy, introducing a cross-cutting pattern —
gets an ADR **before** the code lands.

### Location and format

```
docs/adr/
  0001-record-architecture-decisions.md
  0002-adopt-clean-architecture-and-modular-monolith.md
  0003-cqrs-with-kommand.md
  ...
```

Use the **MADR** template (Markdown ADR), short form:

```markdown
# <NNNN> - <Title>

- Status: proposed | accepted | superseded by ADR-XXXX | deprecated
- Date: YYYY-MM-DD
- Deciders: <names>

## Context and problem statement
What is the situation? What forces are at play?

## Decision drivers
- <driver 1>
- <driver 2>

## Considered options
- Option A
- Option B
- Option C

## Decision outcome
Chosen option: "<A>", because <justification>.

### Consequences
- Positive: ...
- Negative: ...
- Neutral: ...

## Links
- Related ADRs, RFCs, issues, docs
```

### Rules for ADRs

- Numbered sequentially, never re-numbered. To overturn one, write a new
  ADR with status `accepted` that marks the old one `superseded by ADR-NNNN`.
- One decision per ADR. If a PR carries two decisions, write two ADRs.
- Keep them short — two screens max. If you need more, link out.
- ADR-0001 is always "Record architecture decisions" (the meta-ADR
  declaring we use ADRs). Add it as the first thing.

### When to write one

Write an ADR for: introducing/removing a library, changing a layer
boundary, picking a persistence pattern, adopting a cross-cutting
pattern (validation pipeline, outbox, auth scheme), changing how
modules communicate. Do **not** write ADRs for routine code — naming,
small refactors, bug fixes.

### Existing ADRs

The current architectural baseline is recorded in `docs/adr/`. Read
these before proposing changes that touch any of these areas:

| #    | Title                                                        | What it locks in                                        |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| 0001 | Record architecture decisions                                | Use MADR short form, numbered, in `docs/adr/`           |
| 0002 | Adopt Clean Architecture and Modular Monolith                | Per-module `Domain/Application/Infrastructure/Api`      |
| 0003 | Use DDD building blocks inside each module                   | Entities, value objects, aggregates, repositories       |
| 0004 | ~~CQRS as application pattern, dispatched via Kommand~~      | Superseded by ADR-0008 (Wolverine)                      |
| 0005 | Entity Framework Core for persistence, one DbContext / module| Per-module `DbContext`, migrations and configurations   |
| 0006 | Use `Result<T>` for expected failures                        | No exceptions for validation/not-found/conflict         |
| 0007 | Use RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails` for HTTP error responses       | Single error format, mapped from `Error.Category`       |
| 0008 | CQRS dispatched via Wolverine (supersedes ADR-0004)          | WolverineFx for CQRS + in-process messaging; no markers |
| 0009 | Serilog for structured logging                               | `Serilog.AspNetCore` + Console / File / Seq sinks       |
| 0010 | Use UUIDv7 (Guid v7) for aggregate identifiers               | `GuidV7.NewGuid()` today; `Guid.CreateVersion7()` on net9+ |
| 0011 | Unit testing conventions for handlers and validators         | Bogus + immutable Builders + `AsyncLifetimeBase` (EF InMemory) |

When you write the next ADR, increment the number, add a row to this
table in the same PR, and link it from any related sections above.

## What not to do

- Do not start sync work or add sync-shaped abstractions yet.
- Do not introduce a new pattern without an ADR.
- Do not silence analyzer rules to make a warning go away — fix the
  code, or write an ADR explaining why the rule is being downgraded.
- Do not put business logic in controllers or handlers — push it to the
  domain.
- Do not create empty modules / projects "for later." Add structure
  when a real use case needs it.
- Do not bypass `Directory.Packages.props` by hard-coding versions in
  a `.csproj`.
- Do not preserve the starter `Controllers/`, `Models/`, `Dtos/`, or
  `Data/TodoContext.cs` as-is. They are scaffolding; replace them as
  the new modules come in.

  `Method_WhenCondition_ShouldOutcome`.

  are not duplicated in this repo.

input.content:
# Status — 2026-04-20

A snapshot of where the repo is, not an ADR. For the decision record
see `docs/adr/`.

## Landed

`TodoLists` is the first Clean-Architecture / DDD slice and is closed
end-to-end across its CRUD surface.

- Aggregate: `TodoList` now has a private parameterless ctor, public
  ctor, private setters, and behavior methods (`Update`, `AddItem`,
  `RemoveItem`, `Synchronized`). `TodoItem` is still anemic — next.
- CQRS via Wolverine (ADR-0008). Shipped use cases: `CreateTodoList`,
  `UpdateTodoList`, `DeleteTodoList`, `GetTodoList`, `GetTodoLists`.
- Controllers split by side: `TodoListsQueryController` and
  `TodoListsCommandController` under `Controllers/TodoLists/{Queries,Commands}/`.
- `IRepositoryCommand<TodoList>` is backed by the existing
  `RepositoryCommand<TEntity>` base class. There is no concrete
  `IRepositoryQuery<TodoList>` in production yet — the query handlers
  resolve the interface at runtime; handler tests use a test-scoped
  stand-in in `TodoApi.Tests/TestSupport/`.
- Validation runs as a custom Wolverine middleware
  (`TodoApi.Infrastructure.Mediator.ValidationMiddleware`) that
  short-circuits the chain with `Result.Failure(ErrorResult.Validation(...))`.
  No exceptions for expected failures (ADR-0006). Validators live next
  to the command/query (`<UseCase>CommandValidator.cs`).
- HTTP error rendering: `ResultExtensions.ToProblemDetails` emits RFC
  7807 `ProblemDetails` / `ValidationProblemDetails` with `code` and
  `traceId` extensions (ADR-0007).
- Logging on Serilog (ADR-0009).
- Testing conventions shipped (ADR-0011): `AsyncLifetimeBase` over EF
  InMemory, `IBuilder<T>`, `TodoListBuilder` / `TodoItemBuilder`,
  FluentAssertions v6, NSubstitute. Handler + validator tests cover
  every TodoLists use case.
- `TodoItem.TodoListId` bumped to `Guid` so the EF model validates
  against the Guid-keyed `TodoList`; `TodoItemsController` updated to
  match (still starter-style, see below).

## Next

- `TodoItem` aggregate refactor: move it under the `TodoList` aggregate
  as a child entity with no separate repository; rework
  `TodoItemsController` into nested routes; mirror the TodoLists
  command/query/validator/test shape.

## Deferred

- Sync with the external system — not started, scope-gated per the
  "Current focus" note in `CLAUDE.md`.
- Replacing the legacy `TodoContext` / `Migrations/` / `Dtos/` starter
  layout with a module-local `Infrastructure/Persistence/` layout.
- A concrete production `IRepositoryQuery<TodoList>` implementation.
- Integration tests at the HTTP level — externally owned by
  `crunchloop/interview-tests`.

### 3. Paste-ready prompt for the `TodoItem` session

````markdown
# Task: refactor `TodoItem` into a child entity of the `TodoList` aggregate

You are working on a .NET 8 ASP.NET Core API (`TodoApi/`) organized as
a modular monolith with Clean Architecture + DDD + CQRS (Wolverine).
The `TodoLists` module is already closed end-to-end and is the shape
you must mirror.

## 0. Read first, code second

- Read `CLAUDE.md` at the repo root — the "Current focus", the "Target
  architecture" section, and the ADR table. Follow every ADR it
  references. In particular:
  - **ADR-0003** — DDD building blocks; aggregates own behavior.
  - **ADR-0005** — EF Core, one `DbContext` per module, configurations
    via `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>`, not inline `OnModelCreating`.
  - **ADR-0006** — `Result<T>` for expected failures, exceptions only
    for the truly exceptional.
  - **ADR-0008** — CQRS via Wolverine, handler convention, no marker
    interfaces. Validation runs as Wolverine middleware.
  - **ADR-0011** — unit testing conventions. Tests ship in the same PR.
- Study the existing `TodoLists` module before writing anything; the
  new `TodoItem` work must mirror its shape:
  - `TodoApi/Application/Commands/<UseCase>/` (command, handler,
    validator, response) and `Application/Queries/<UseCase>/`.
  - `TodoApi/Controllers/TodoLists/{Commands,Queries}/` — split
    per side.
  - `TodoApi/Data/Entities/TodoList.cs` — aggregate shape.
  - `TodoApi.Tests/Application/…`, `TodoApi.Tests/TestSupport/`,
    `TodoApi.Tests/Builders/` — test convention.

## 1. DDD shape (not up for debate)

- `TodoList` is the aggregate root.
- `TodoItem` is a **child entity** — a collection inside `TodoList`,
  reached only through the aggregate root.
- **No** `ITodoItemRepository`. Items are added / updated / completed /
  removed through `TodoList` via methods on the aggregate
  (`AddItem`, `CompleteItem`, `UpdateItem`, `RemoveItem`) that enforce
  invariants. The existing `TodoList.AddItem` / `RemoveItem` methods
  are starting points — extend as needed.
- EF Core: one-to-many navigation, cascade delete when the parent list
  is deleted. Configure in an `IEntityTypeConfiguration<TodoList>` (or
  a sibling for items), not inline in `OnModelCreating`.
- Commands and queries are plain records. Handlers are Wolverine
  handlers named `<UseCase>Handler` with a `Handle` method returning
  `Result<T>` or `Result`.

## 2. Scope (do exactly these, nothing more)

**Commands / queries** under `TodoApi/Application/`:

- `Application/Commands/AddTodoItem/` — request carries `TodoListId`
  and the item payload.
- `Application/Commands/CompleteTodoItem/`
- `Application/Commands/UpdateTodoItem/`
- `Application/Commands/RemoveTodoItem/`
- `Application/Queries/GetTodoItems/` — items for a given list.

Each folder contains the command / query record, the handler, a
validator where it buys anything (use error codes namespaced
`todoitems.<field>.<rule>`), and a response record.

**HTTP endpoints** nested under the parent list. Follow the same
`Controllers/TodoLists/{Commands,Queries}/` split pattern adapted for
`TodoItems`:

- `POST   /todolists/{listId:guid}/items`
- `PUT    /todolists/{listId:guid}/items/{itemId}`
- `DELETE /todolists/{listId:guid}/items/{itemId}`
- `GET` and `CompleteTodoItem` routes: mirror the equivalent TodoLists
  shapes (e.g. `GET /todolists/{listId:guid}/items`, and a
  `CompleteTodoItem` route that reads naturally — `PUT .../items/{itemId}/complete`
  is fine). If a sensible analog isn't obvious, flag and ask rather
  than invent.

Controllers only translate HTTP → command/query and map the resulting
`Result<T>` via `ResultExtensions` (`.ToOk()`, `.ToCreated()`,
`.ToNoContent()`, `.ToProblemDetails()`). **No** `IValidator<T>`
injection, no `ModelState` checks, no hand-built `ProblemDetails` —
validation runs through the existing Wolverine `ValidationMiddleware`.

Delete or retire `TodoApi/Controllers/TodoItems/TodoItemsController.cs`
and its starter DTOs in `TodoApi/Dtos/` (CreateTodoItem / UpdateTodoItem)
once the new endpoints cover their surface.

## 3. Tests (same PR, not a follow-up)

Per **ADR-0011**:

- Handler tests inherit `AsyncLifetimeBase`, seed via
  `TodoListBuilder` / `TodoItemBuilder` (update `TodoItemBuilder` if
  the entity shape changes). Construct the real handler with the real
  repository + real `DbContext`. Mock `IClock` (and only `IClock`)
  via NSubstitute. Assert on `Result<T>` first (`IsSuccess` or
  `Error.Definition.Should().Be(ErrorDefinition.NotFound)`), then
  re-read persisted state with `AsNoTracking()`.
- Validator tests use `FluentValidation.TestHelper` (no base class),
  builders for payloads, `[Theory]` + `[InlineData]` for boundaries,
  `[Fact]` for one-offs. Assert `.WithErrorCode(...)` on the
  namespaced codes.
- Assertions use FluentAssertions (v6). No `Assert.Equal` in new code.
- Naming: `Method_WhenCondition_ShouldOutcome`.
- Mirror the production folder tree inside `TodoApi.Tests/`:
  `Application/Commands/<UseCase>/<UseCase>HandlerTests.cs`,
  `<UseCase>CommandValidatorTests.cs`, and the Queries equivalents.

## 4. Out of scope

- No sync / outbox work.
- No new ADRs.
- No renames of the existing `TodoLists` surface.
- No integration tests / `WebApplicationFactory` / HTTP-level tests

- No relocation of `TodoContext` / migrations.
- No production changes outside what this refactor requires.
````
